$comment: Committed deterministic snapshot of eval/baseline-selector.mjs's exact output against the live 24-tool registry + eval-tasks.json v2 (11 tasks), captured 2026-07-20 after the Tools-v2 Option B scaffold added novada_scrape_amazon (T11 + registry + collision baseline). Tier A (bag-of-words) is FULLY DETERMINISTIC — no key, no network, no model sampling — so this snapshot is exact and safe to gate against byte-for-byte. baseline-selector.mjs gates as REGRESSION-VS-THIS-BASELINE (FIX 2, gate-hardening pass): fail if exact_accuracy drops below the value below, OR a NEW forbidden hit appears (forbidden_hits grows beyond the set below), OR a task listed here with correct:true now scores correct:false. Do NOT gate on forbidden_hits.length===0 — the 1 recorded forbidden hit (T03) is expected weak-selector noise (a bag-of-words heuristic confusing novada_search with novada_research), not a defect to chase to zero; see baseline-selector.mjs's BASELINE_FLOOR doc and eval-tasks.json's own task notes. Update this file deliberately (new date + note why) whenever eval-tasks.json or the registry's tool descriptions change on purpose — never regenerate it just to make a red run go green. 2026-07-20 UPDATE: adding novada_scrape_amazon (name/desc tokens 'amazon'/'scrape'/'product'/'listings') changed T04's Tier-A pick from novada_search (forbidden) to novada_scrape_amazon (not forbidden, still not the expected novada_scrape) — net effect: T04 stays correct:false (no true->false regression) and drops OUT of forbidden_hits (an improvement, not a new hit); T11 (new, Amazon+ASIN prompt) is a clean correct:true pick for the new tool. Exact accuracy moved 50%->55% (5/10->6/11). Verified via `npm run build && node eval/baseline-selector.mjs --json`. 2026-07-20 UPDATE 2 (Amazon-scaffold hardening, FIX 2): T04's acceptable_tools/sibling_watch were widened to include novada_scrape_amazon (it is now an equally-correct pick for that Amazon-specific prompt, not just novada_scrape). Regenerated via the same documented command and confirmed the delta HONESTLY: this is a ZERO-delta regen for every field gated/recorded below — exact_accuracy, forbidden_hits, and every per_task.correct value (including T04's, still false) are byte-identical to the prior snapshot. That is expected, not a bug in the regen: baseline-selector.mjs's `correct` and `forbidden_hit` computations are strict equality against expected_tool / membership in unacceptable_tools — neither reads acceptable_tools at all, so widening acceptable_tools cannot move any field this file records or evaluateRegressionGate checks. The only thing that changed under the hood is the non-persisted `acceptable_accuracy` metric (T04 now scores acceptable:true instead of false, since novada_scrape_amazon is what Tier-A already picks for it) — that metric is reported by baseline-selector.mjs's live run but was never part of this committed snapshot's schema, so no field below moves. Re-verified via `npm run build && node eval/baseline-selector.mjs --json`.
